relations: Senet originated in Ancient Egypt around 3100 BC
The Royal Game of Ur was played in ancient Mesopotamia around 2600 BC
Go is one of the oldest board games still played today, originating from the Zhou Dynasty in China
Go was developed during the Zhou Dynasty in ancient China
Xiangqi, also known as Chinese Chess, has its origins in ancient China
Both Chaturanga and Backgammon are ancient games that evolved over time and spread across cultures
Both emerged during the Renaissance and were played by people across different social classes
Both games became household names due to mass production in the 19th and 20th centuries
Monopoly is an example of a board game that has become a household name
Both Scrabble and Clue were popularized through advancements in printing technology and the Industrial Revolution
Scrabble is an example of a board game, known for its word-based gameplay
Clue is an example of a board game, recognized for its mystery-themed gameplay
Tabletop gaming cafes contribute to the popularity and community of board games
Board game conventions are events that celebrate and promote board games
Online platforms facilitate the playing of board games digitally, expanding the community
